DI-DS-001.rationale: Same method: yes/no Data Sufficiency on an inequality decided by sign preservation under powers -- DI-DS-005's x^2>y^2 statement is the identical 'even power destroys sign information' trap, mirroring x^2>0 being insufficient while the odd power x^3>0 is decisive.

DI-DS-002.rationale: Same method: value-type Data Sufficiency where one statement's constraint pins a unique value while the other allows infinitely many (answer A) -- DI-DS-006 is structurally identical (x+4=11 fixes x, 'positive integer' does not).

DI-DS-003.rationale: Same method: yes/no Data Sufficiency resolved by a parity/power lemma (k^2 even iff k even; odd*k parity equals k parity) -- DI-DS-001 uses the same 'a power or odd multiplier preserves or hides a property' deduction to settle the yes/no question.

DI-DS-004.rationale: Same method: set up and solve a 2x2 system of linear equations, needing both a total and a relation to pin two unknowns -- DI-TPA-002 (two supply types, total units + total cost) uses the identical two-equations-for-two-unknowns approach the DS question needs when combining statements to C.

DI-DS-005.rationale: Same method: yes/no Data Sufficiency on an inequality where squaring loses order information (x^2>y^2 only gives |x|>|y|) -- DI-DS-001 turns on the same 'even power hides sign' trap while a direct statement resolves it.

DI-DS-006.rationale: Same method: value-type Data Sufficiency where a single linear/constraint statement determines a unique value and a loose condition allows many (answer A) -- DI-DS-002 has the identical 'one statement fixes the value, the other permits infinitely many' structure.

DI-GI-001.rationale: Same method: scan a series/set of categories, compute the per-item change or derived metric, and select the extreme (the largest increase); DI-TA-001 is the same 'largest increase across categories' comparison on a table.

DI-GI-101.rationale: Same method: compute a ratio/derived metric per category (margin = profit/revenue vs net = units*price*(1-return)) and pick the max, with the identical trap that the largest absolute value is not the largest metric.

DI-MSR-001.rationale: Same method: synthesize facts from two sources and apply all stated policy conditions to a specific case to reach a yes/no determination; DI-MSR-101 is the same multi-source rule-application problem.

DI-MSR-101.rationale: Same method: integrate two sources and apply a stated policy (compute the discounted total, then test it against a threshold) to determine qualification; DI-MSR-001 is the same multi-source condition-checking method.

DI-TA-001.rationale: Same method: compute the per-category change/metric and select the extreme; DI-GI-001 is the same 'find the largest increase across data points' comparison, on a chart series rather than a table.

DI-TA-101.rationale: Same method: compute a derived metric per row (net = units*price*(1-return rate)) and pick the max; DI-GI-101 uses the identical compute-metric-then-select-max approach with the same 'biggest gross/absolute is not biggest metric' trap.

DI-TPA-001.rationale: Same method: set up and solve a system of two linear equations from a total-count constraint plus a total-value constraint; DI-TPA-002 is the identical count-plus-cost system solved by substitution.

DI-TPA-002.rationale: DI-TPA-001 uses the same method: build two simultaneous linear equations from a total-count and a total-value constraint and solve by substitution.

DI-TPA-003.rationale: Q-PS-013 uses the same combined-work-rate method: add individual per-hour rates to get a combined rate, then divide total output by it.

Q-CR-101.rationale: V-CR-001 uses the same method: weaken a causal claim by introducing an alternative/confounding cause acting in the same period.

Q-CR-102.rationale: V-CR-003 uses the same method: strengthen a causal claim by supplying evidence that ties cause to effect and rules out confounds/coincidence.

Q-CR-103.rationale: V-CR-004 uses the same method: a must-be-true deduction chaining categorical/conditional statements to eliminate impossible cases.

Q-PS-001.rationale: Q-PS-101 uses the same method: multiply successive percent-change factors in sequence rather than adding the percentages.

Q-PS-002.rationale: Q-PS-108 uses the same method: solve a system of two linear equations in two unknowns by elimination.

Q-PS-003.rationale: Same method: prime-factorize the number and reason about the exponents of each prime (2 and 3) to determine divisibility, matching Q-PS-107's 12^3 = 2^6 * 3^3 exponent decomposition.

Q-PS-004.rationale: Same method: combine fractions over a common denominator to compute a fractional part/difference of a whole, as in Q-PS-021's 4/5 - 3/5 = 1/5 of capacity.

Q-PS-005.rationale: Same method: set up a part-to-part ratio, find the value of one 'part' from a known quantity, then scale, exactly as Q-PS-018 does with the 5:2 flour/sugar recipe.

Q-PS-006.rationale: Same method: rewrite the terms to a common base and equate/manipulate exponents to solve, matching Q-PS-020's rewriting of 4^x and 8^(x-1) in base 2.

Q-PS-007.rationale: Same method: convert a given arithmetic mean into the total sum (mean x count) and back out the unknown value, as Q-PS-103 does with sum = 100 from mean 20 over 5 numbers.

Q-PS-008.rationale: Same concept: compute descriptive statistics (median/range) from an ordered list, matching Q-PS-103's reasoning about the median of a set of numbers.

Q-PS-009.rationale: Same method: factor a quadratic expression into linear factors and set them to zero to find the solution values, as Q-PS-105 factors x^2 - y^2 = 45 into (x-y)(x+y).

Q-PS-010.rationale: Q-PS-104 shares the method: solve an absolute-value inequality and count the integers in the resulting solution interval.

Q-PS-011.rationale: Q-PS-006 shares the method: substitute a value into a given algebraic expression and evaluate/combine to a numeric result.

Q-PS-012.rationale: Q-PS-013 shares the rate-aggregation method: combine per-unit rates and use total quantity over total time rather than a simple average.

Q-PS-013.rationale: Q-PS-109 shares the method: add individual work rates (reciprocals of times) to get a combined rate and invert for time.

Q-PS-014.rationale: Q-PS-015 shares the method: a single percent-of-a-base computation, forming and solving one linear percent relationship.

Q-PS-015.rationale: Q-PS-001 shares the method: treat a percent discount as a multiplicative factor (1 - rate) on a base price and solve via that factor.

Q-PS-016.rationale: Q-PS-107 shares the method: use divisor/prime-factorization structure of an integer (enumerating its factors) to compute the answer.

Q-PS-017.rationale: Same method: translate a word problem into a linear equation/system in defined unknowns and solve algebraically (the digit relation reduces to solving a linear equation for the digit).

Q-PS-018.rationale: Same method: proportional scaling of a ratio - given one quantity in an a:b ratio, apply the scale factor / unit-part value to find the other.

Q-PS-019.rationale: Same method: simplify/evaluate a powers-and-roots expression by rewriting radicals/powers to a common base using laws of exponents and roots.

Q-PS-020.rationale: Same method: solve an exponential relation by expressing both sides in the same (prime) base and equating the exponents.

Q-PS-021.rationale: Same method: a known amount equals a fractional/part-of-whole share - set (fraction or unit part) x whole = amount and solve for the total.

Q-PS-101.rationale: Same method: apply successive percent-change multipliers in sequence (chain the factors), with the classic 'the changes do not cancel' trap.

Q-PS-102.rationale: Q-PS-005: both set a ratio's parts equal to a known quantity and scale to solve for an unknown amount (ratio-parts / proportion method).

Q-PS-103.rationale: Q-PS-007: both convert the arithmetic mean into a fixed sum constraint and solve for a value under that constraint (mean-as-sum reasoning).

Q-PS-104.rationale: Q-PS-010: both solve an absolute-value inequality by converting it to a bounded interval and counting the integers strictly inside it.

Q-PS-105.rationale: Q-PS-106: both factor the equation into a product equal to a constant and count integer factor pairs subject to an ordering/distinctness constraint (Diophantine factor-pair counting).

Q-PS-106.rationale: Q-PS-105: both clear/rearrange into a product equal to a constant and enumerate integer factor pairs to count valid solutions (Diophantine factor-pair counting).

Q-PS-107.rationale: Q-PS-020: both rewrite the terms with a common prime base and equate exponents to solve for the unknowns (prime-factorization / equate-exponents method).

Q-PS-108.rationale: Q-PS-002: both solve a 2x2 system of linear equations by elimination to find the unit prices (linear-system elimination).

Q-PS-109.rationale: Q-PS-013 uses the same work-rate method: add individual per-hour rates (1/6 + 1/9) to get a combined rate, exactly the mechanic the query's first phase relies on.

Q-PS-110.rationale: Q-PS-003 shares the same integer-property / 'must be true' method: reason about the prime-factor (mod/parity) structure forced by a perfect square to derive what necessarily holds for n.

V-CR-001.rationale: Q-CR-101 uses the identical weaken-a-causal-claim method: introduce a confounding alternative cause acting in the same time and place to undercut the 'lights caused the drop' inference.

V-CR-002.rationale: V-CR-008 is the same required-assumption method: identify the unstated condition a plan depends on and confirm it via the negation test (savings/pass-rates not offset).

V-CR-003.rationale: Q-CR-102 uses the same strengthen-a-causal-claim method: add evidence (a randomized control) that ties the proposed cause to the effect and rules out coincidence/confounds.

V-CR-004.rationale: Q-CR-103 uses the same formal-logic 'must be true' method: chain universal conditional statements to deduce the single forced conclusion while rejecting reversals and overstatements.

V-CR-005.rationale: V-CR-002 shares the evaluate/plan-assumption method: pin down the unstated fact that most determines whether the plan's predicted outcome actually holds.

V-CR-006.rationale: V-CR-104 is the same Resolve-the-Paradox CR method: reconcile a surprising drop (revenue falls despite a price cut / speed unchanged despite fewer cars) by supplying the missing offsetting variable rather than attacking the argument.

V-CR-007.rationale: V-RC-004 uses the same Role/Function-of-a-Statement method: identify what a specific sentence does in the argument's structure (the boldface line / the 'nearly disappearing vs surging' detail) rather than judge its truth.

V-CR-008.rationale: V-CR-002 is the same Required-Assumption method: find the unstated premise the plan depends on and confirm it via the negation test (online pass rates hold / cost savings not offset by lost revenue).

V-CR-009.rationale: V-CR-001 uses the same Weaken-a-Causal-Claim method: sever the link between the cited evidence and the 'X caused Y' conclusion (speed measures tastiness not nutrition / an alternative cause explains the accident drop).

V-CR-104.rationale: V-CR-006 is the same Resolve-the-Paradox CR method: explain an apparently contradictory result (same speed despite fewer cars / lower revenue despite a price cut) by introducing an overlooked offsetting factor.

V-RC-001.rationale: V-RC-102 uses the same RC method of retrieving a stated explanatory mechanism/condition from the passage (why rereading persists / when choice-overload shrinks), matching the answer to what the text supports rather than analyzing an argument.

V-RC-002.rationale: V-RC-101 is the same RC Main-Idea method: pick the option capturing the passage's central claim in full while rejecting too-narrow or overstated distortions.

V-RC-003.rationale: Explicit-detail retrieval ('According to the passage' fact lookup) — V-RC-102 is the other 'According to the passage... ' direct-statement question.

V-RC-004.rationale: Function/purpose of a cited detail — no other 'in order to' item exists, so the nearest are interpretive passage-reasoning items; V-RC-001 (inference tying evidence to claim) is the closest cognitive match.

V-RC-005.rationale: Author's attitude/tone toward a claim — V-RC-104 is the other 'author's attitude ... best described as' measured-stance question.

V-RC-101.rationale: Main idea / main point of the passage — V-RC-002 is the parallel 'best expresses the main idea' question.

V-RC-102.rationale: Explicit-detail retrieval ('According to the passage' fact lookup) — V-RC-003 is the other 'According to the passage' direct-statement question.

V-RC-103.rationale: Inference from the passage ('most strongly implies') — V-RC-001 ('the passage suggests ... best explained by') is the parallel inference question.

V-RC-104.rationale: Author's attitude/tone toward scholarship — V-RC-005 is the other 'author's attitude ... best described as' measured-stance question.
